question: [File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _triggerBorrowingFee] Can an unprivileged SABLE staker under preconditions of an active stake in SableStakingV2 and a pending openTrove/withdrawUSDS transaction visible in the mempool trigger stake(largeAmount) → wait for borrow tx to execute _triggerBorrowingFee (which calls sableStaking.increaseF_USDS(USDSFee) then usdsToken.mint(sableStakingAddress, USDSFee)) → unstake(largeAmount), violating the invariant that each staker's share of F_USDS rewards must be proportional to their time-weighted stake and not inflated by just-in-time stake insertion, causing scoped impact: staker claims a disproportionately large share of the USDS borrowing fee reward relative to long-term stakers with equal or greater stake? Local proof idea: add a Foundry fuzz test with two stakers A (long-term) and B (JIT), parameterize borrow amount and JIT stake size, call stake(large) before borrow, borrow executes _triggerBorrowingFee, call unstake, assert B.USDSGain / A.USDSGain > B.stakeTime / A.stakeTime.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _triggerBorrowingFee] Can an unprivileged staker under preconditions of a non-zero stake in SableStakingV2 and a reentrancy-capable receive() or fallback in the staking contract trigger sableStaking.increaseF_USDS(USDSFee) (line 610) → reenter BorrowerOperations or SableStakingV2 to claim pending USDS gain → usdsToken.mint(sableStakingAddress, USDSFee) (line 611) executes after claim, violating the invariant that USDS must be minted to the staking contract before any staker can claim it, causing scoped impact: staker claims USDS fee reward before the tokens exist in the staking contract, draining other stakers' claimable balance? Local proof idea: deploy a malicious SableStakingV2 stub that calls unstake() inside increaseF_USDS(), run openTrove, assert staking contract USDS balance goes negative or staker receives more than minted.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: openTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of TCR < CCR (system in recovery mode, _checkRecoveryMode returns true) trigger openTrove with valid ICR >= CCR, violating the invariant that sableStaking.increaseF_USDS and usdsToken.mint(sableStakingAddress, fee) must be called for every USDS issuance event so stakers receive their entitled fee share, causing scoped impact: borrower mints USDS debt without paying any borrowing fee to SABLE stakers, depriving stakers of reward value they are entitled to under normal-mode borrowing? Local proof idea: add a unit test that sets TCR just below CCR, calls openTrove, asserts sableStaking.getPendingUSDSGain(staker) == 0 and USDSBorrowingFeePaid event emits 0, then compare against normal-mode borrow of same size.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _adjustTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of an active trove, isDebtIncrease=true, and system in recovery mode (isRecoveryMode=true) trigger adjustTrove with USDSChange > 0 and newICR >= CCR and newICR >= oldICR, violating the invariant at line 461 (if (adjustTroveParam.isDebtIncrease && !isRecoveryMode)) that the fee branch is skipped in recovery mode, causing scoped impact: borrower increases USDS debt in recovery mode without triggering _triggerBorrowingFee, so sableStaking.increaseF_USDS is never called and stakers receive zero fee reward for this debt issuance? Local proof idea: fuzz test with isRecoveryMode=true, isDebtIncrease=true, assert USDSBorrowingFeePaid emits 0 and sableStaking F_USDS accumulator is unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _adjustTrove / _moveTokensAndBNBfromAdjustment] Can an unprivileged actor under preconditions of being the StabilityPool (msg.sender == stabilityPoolAddress) calling moveBNBGainToTrove for a borrower with an active trove trigger _adjustTrove(_borrower, ...) where moveParam.borrower is set to msg.sender (line 544: borrower: msg.sender) instead of _borrower, violating the invariant that USDS minted or BNB sent in _moveTokensAndBNBfromAdjustment must go to the trove owner (_borrower) not the caller (msg.sender), causing scoped impact: if a future code path allows isDebtIncrease=true with msg.sender != _borrower, USDS would be minted to msg.sender (StabilityPool or attacker) instead of the trove owner, enabling unauthorized USDS extraction? Local proof idea: add a differential unit test comparing _borrower vs msg.sender as USDS mint recipient across all _adjustTrove call paths, assert usdsToken.balanceOf(_borrower) increases and usdsToken.balanceOf(msg.sender) does not when msg.sender != _borrower.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: openTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of a fresh trove open trigger openTrove where updateTroveRewardSnapshots(msg.sender) is called at line 241 (after increaseTroveDebt but before updateStakeAndTotalStakes at line 242), violating the invariant that reward snapshots must be taken after stake is finalized so the trove's pending redistribution reward baseline is correct, causing scoped impact: if L_BNB or L_USDSDebt accumulators advance between snapshot and stake update, the new trove's pending reward calculation is based on a stale snapshot, allowing it to claim redistribution rewards it did not earn? Local proof idea: add an invariant test that opens a trove, advances L_BNB via a liquidation between snapshot and stake update (simulated via mock), then calls applyPendingRewards and asserts pendingBNBReward == 0 for the new trove.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _adjustTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of an active trove with pending redistribution rewards (hasPendingRewards returns true) trigger adjustTrove where applyPendingRewards(_borrower) is called at line 443 before getTroveDebt/getTroveColl at lines 466-467, violating the invariant that the trove's debt and collateral read after applyPendingRewards must include all redistributed amounts so ICR and fee calculations are based on the true post-reward state, causing scoped impact: if applyPendingRewards does not atomically update TroveManager storage before the debt/coll reads, the ICR check uses stale values, allowing a borrower to withdraw collateral or increase debt beyond what the true post-reward ICR permits? Local proof idea: fuzz test with large pending BNB and USDS rewards, call adjustTrove with collWithdrawal near the pre-reward limit, assert newICR post-adjustment is still >= MCR using the true post-reward debt.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _triggerBorrowingFee / _requireUserAcceptsFee] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of a very small _USDSAmount (e.g., equal to systemState.getMinNetDebt()) trigger openTrove or withdrawUSDS where _requireUserAcceptsFee computes feePercentage = USDSFee.mul(DECIMAL_PRECISION).div(USDSAmount) and the result truncates to zero due to integer division, violating the invariant that the fee percentage must be >= BORROWING_FEE_FLOOR so stakers always receive a minimum fee per unit of USDS issued, causing scoped impact: borrower passes the fee check with an effective fee rate below the floor, reducing the USDS reward minted to sableStakingAddress below the protocol-guaranteed minimum? Local proof idea: fuzz _USDSAmount from 1 to MIN_NET_DEBT, compute expected fee via getBorrowingFee, assert feePercentage = fee * 1e18 / USDSAmount >= BORROWING_FEE_FLOOR for all inputs.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _triggerBorrowingFee] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of a high baseRate that has not been decayed for a long time trigger openTrove where decayBaseRateFromBorrowing() is called at line 604 before getBorrowingFee(USDSAmount, oracleRate) at line 605, violating the invariant that the fee used to compute the staker reward must reflect the pre-decay rate at the time of the transaction (not the post-decay rate), causing scoped impact: borrower benefits from the decay applied within the same transaction, paying a lower fee to stakers than the rate that was in effect when the transaction was submitted? Local proof idea: add a unit test that sets baseRate to 5%, advances time by 1 hour, calls openTrove, asserts USDSFee < USDSAmount * 5% (pre-decay rate) and equals USDSAmount * decayedRate, then compare staker reward to expected pre-decay reward.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: openTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of a system where oracleRateCalc.getOracleRate returns 0 (e.g., publishTimePyth is recent and deviationPyth is zero) trigger openTrove where getBorrowingFee(USDSAmount, 0) is called, violating the invariant that the borrowing fee must be at least BORROWING_FEE_FLOOR * USDSAmount so stakers always receive a minimum reward per borrow, causing scoped impact: if getBorrowingFee with oracleRate=0 returns a fee below the floor or zero, stakers receive less USDS reward than the protocol guarantees per unit of USDS issued? Local proof idea: deploy a mock OracleRateCalculation that returns 0, call openTrove, assert USDSBorrowingFeePaid >= USDSAmount * BORROWING_FEE_FLOOR / 1e18.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: openTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of a system where systemState.getUSDSGasCompensation() returns a value that changes between the openTrove call and a subsequent closeTrove call trigger openTrove (compositeDebt = netDebt + gasComp_at_open) then closeTrove (repays debt - gasComp_at_close from user, gasComp_at_close from gasPool), violating the invariant that the gas compensation burned on close must equal the gas compensation minted on open so the gasPool balance is always solvent, causing scoped impact: if gasComp increases between open and close, the gasPool is drained of more USDS than was minted for this trove, creating a deficit that reduces gas compensation available to liquidators? Local proof idea: add an invariant test that opens a trove, changes USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION via systemState, closes the trove, asserts gasPool USDS balance >= 0 and equals sum of all open trove gas compensations.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: openTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of a normal-mode system trigger openTrove where _withdrawUSDS is called twice (lines 267 and 278) with two separate activePool.increaseUSDSDebt calls (netDebt and USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION), violating the invariant that total activePool.getUSDSDebt() increase must equal exactly compositeDebt = netDebt + USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION for each trove opened, causing scoped impact: if the two increaseUSDSDebt calls are not atomic and an external actor reads activePool debt between them, the system debt is temporarily understated, potentially allowing a concurrent redemption or liquidation to use a stale TCR that permits extraction of more collateral than the true system state allows? Local proof idea: add a reentrancy test that reads activePool.getUSDSDebt() inside a mock activePool.increaseUSDSDebt callback, assert the intermediate value equals netDebt (not compositeDebt) and verify no redemption can exploit this window.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: closeTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of an active trove with pending redistribution rewards trigger closeTrove where applyPendingRewards(msg.sender) at line 568 is called before getTroveColl/getTroveDebt at lines 570-571, violating the invariant that the coll and debt read for repayment must include all pending redistribution rewards so the borrower repays the full true debt and receives the full true collateral, causing scoped impact: if applyPendingRewards does not fully settle pending BNB and USDS rewards before the reads, the borrower repays less than their true debt and receives less collateral than their true entitlement, or conversely receives more collateral than they paid for? Local proof idea: add a unit test with large pending rewards, call closeTrove, assert usdsToken burned == trueDebt - gasComp and BNB received == trueColl.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: closeTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of an active trove trigger closeTrove where _repayUSDS(activePoolCached, usdsTokenCached, gasPoolAddress, USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION) at line 587 burns USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION from gasPoolAddress, violating the invariant that the gasPool must always hold at least USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION for every active trove so liquidators can always be compensated, causing scoped impact: if multiple troves are closed concurrently or if gasPool balance is insufficient due to a prior accounting error, the burn reverts or the gasPool is over-drained, depriving liquidators of their gas compensation reward? Local proof idea: add an invariant test that opens N troves, closes K of them, asserts gasPool.balanceOf >= (N-K) * USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION after each close.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: claimCollateral] Can an unprivileged user under preconditions of a zero collateral surplus balance in CollSurplusPool trigger claimCollateral() which calls collSurplusPool.claimColl(msg.sender) at line 598, violating the invariant that claimColl must revert or return 0 if the caller has no surplus so no BNB is transferred to an unauthorized recipient, causing scoped impact: if CollSurplusPool.claimColl does not revert on zero balance and instead sends 0 BNB (no-op), a user can call claimCollateral repeatedly without effect, but if there is a rounding or accounting bug in CollSurplusPool that credits a non-zero amount on repeated zero-balance claims, the attacker extracts BNB? Local proof idea: add a fuzz test calling claimCollateral N times with zero surplus balance, assert BNB balance of caller does not increase and CollSurplusPool BNB balance is unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _adjustTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of an active trove in normal mode trigger adjustTrove with isDebtIncrease=false and USDSChange = _getNetDebt(vars.debt) (maximum repayment) where _requireAtLeastMinNetDebt(_getNetDebt(vars.debt).sub(vars.netDebtChange)) at line 489 is checked, violating the invariant that after repayment the trove's net debt must be >= MIN_NET_DEBT or the trove must be fully closed, causing scoped impact: if the subtraction underflows (SafeMath reverts) or if the check passes when netDebt - repayment < MIN_NET_DEBT due to rounding, the trove is left in a dust state that cannot be liquidated or closed normally, trapping collateral and distorting reward accounting? Local proof idea: fuzz USDSChange from 0 to getNetDebt(debt), assert either the tx reverts with 'net debt must be greater than minimum' or the resulting net debt >= MIN_NET_DEBT.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _adjustTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of an active trove trigger adjustTrove with isDebtIncrease=false and USDSChange > 0 where _requireValidUSDSRepayment checks _debtRepayment <= _currentDebt.sub(USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION) at line 820, violating the invariant that a borrower cannot repay more than their net debt (debt minus gas compensation) in a single adjustTrove call so the gas compensation always remains in the trove's debt, causing scoped impact: if _currentDebt < USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION (impossible under normal invariants but possible after accounting corruption), SafeMath sub reverts, or if the check is bypassed, the borrower burns more USDS than their net debt and the activePool debt goes negative? Local proof idea: add a unit test with _currentDebt = USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION + 1, USDSChange = 2, assert tx reverts with 'Amount repaid must not be larger than the Trove's debt'.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _adjustTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of an active trove trigger adjustTrove with both msg.value > 0 and collWithdrawal > 0 where _requireSingularCollChange at line 428 checks msg.value == 0 || collWithdrawal == 0, violating the invariant that a single adjustment cannot simultaneously add and withdraw collateral so the collChange direction is unambiguous, causing scoped impact: if the require is bypassed (e.g., via a delegatecall or proxy that modifies msg.value), the attacker could add collateral and withdraw collateral in the same tx, potentially double-counting the collateral change in _getCollChange and extracting more BNB than deposited? Local proof idea: add a unit test that calls _adjustTrove with msg.value=1 ether and collWithdrawal=1 ether, assert it reverts with 'Cannot withdraw and add coll'.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _adjustTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of an active trove in recovery mode trigger adjustTrove with isDebtIncrease=true and newICR >= CCR and newICR >= oldICR where _requireValidMaxFeePercentage at line 425 only checks maxFeePercentage <= DECIMAL_PRECISION (no floor check in recovery mode per lines 844-847), violating the invariant that even in recovery mode a debt increase must pay at least BORROWING_FEE_FLOOR to stakers, causing scoped impact: since _triggerBorrowingFee is skipped entirely in recovery mode (line 461), the maxFeePercentage floor check is irrelevant and stakers receive zero fee for debt increases in recovery mode regardless of maxFeePercentage? Local proof idea: add a state-transition test that enters recovery mode, calls adjustTrove with isDebtIncrease=true, asserts sableStaking.increaseF_USDS was never called and F_USDS accumulator is unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/BorrowerOperations.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _adjustTrove] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions of an active trove in recovery mode trigger adjustTrove with collWithdrawal > 0 where _requireNoCollWithdrawal at line 762 should revert, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal is forbidden in recovery mode to protect system solvency, causing scoped impact: if _requireNoCollWithdrawal is bypassed (e.g., via a path where isRecoveryMode is computed differently between the check and the actual state), the borrower extracts BNB collateral during recovery mode, worsening the TCR and potentially triggering cascading liquidations that harm StabilityPool depositors' BNB gain entitlement? Local proof idea: add a fuzz test that sets TCR just below CCR, calls adjustTrove with collWithdrawal=1, assert it reverts with 'Collateral withdrawal not permitted Recovery Mode'.
